data.title: Musk Blasts OpenAI’s $800 Billion Closed-Source Shift
data.description: Elon Musk said OpenAI has become an $800 billion closed-source company, calling it the “exact opposite” of the nonprofit he funded and saying his dispute with Sam Altman is about mission, not personality. According to BeInCrypto, Musk made the remarks in an interview with The Economist's Zanny Minton Beddoes and said the Anthropic team left OpenAI because it did not trust Altman. He also said Dario Amodei is “a very principled person,” and added that OpenAI’s shift from its founding mission is his main grievance.

data.title: China Publishes Tougher Solar and Polysilicon Energy-Efficiency Standards
data.description: On July 22, China published the detailed rules for three mandatory national standards covering energy consumption limits for polysilicon and germanium unit products, energy-efficiency limits and grades for crystalline silicon PV modules and inverters, and energy consumption limits for monocrystalline silicon unit products, with implementation set for January 1, 2027. Jiemian News reported that Huatai Securities said the energy-efficiency requirements for polysilicon were stricter than expected, with the grade-3 standard tightened versus the September 16, 2025 draft. Huatai Securities said the mandatory standards will raise entry barriers across polysilicon, wafers, cells and modules, and reaffirmed its view that solar demand-side pressure may ease in the second half of 2026 while the new standards could speed supply-side consolidation in 2027.

data.title: China’s CSRC Says Capital Markets Have Good Allocation Value
data.description: China's Securities Regulatory Commission (CSRC) said at a July 23 meeting that geopolitical conflicts remain elevated and risks of global financial market spillovers are rising, while positive factors are accumulating in macro policy, fundamentals and liquidity. According to Jiemian News, the commission also said reforms in China's capital markets are continuing to take effect and that the market as a whole has relatively good allocation value.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Modi Seeks to Calm India Protest Anger Over Exam Paper Leaks
data.description:
Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi made a fresh effort to calm anger over examination paper leaks as students prepared nationwide protests on Friday, according to Bloomberg.

Demands are growing for the education minister to step down as the backlash widens.

data.title: Niche Holdings, Wai Kee To Buy Targets For Up To HK$100 Million
data.description:
Niche Holdings and Wai Kee Holdings said they have entered into a restructuring framework agreement with the target companies, the seller and the provisional liquidators acting as seller, to acquire about 100% interests in the target companies, including Paul Y. Construction, for a maximum total consideration of HK$100 million, according to Ming Pao.

After completion, the target companies and the investor or its nominee will retain only their licences and the "Paul Y." trademark, and will no longer hold any other substantive or material assets or liabilities.

data.title: HD Korea Shipbuilding Signs AI Platform Deal With Siemens Unit
data.description: According to Yonhap, HD Korea Shipbuilding & Offshore Engineering Co. (HD KSOE) said Friday it signed an agreement with Siemens Digital Industries Software to adopt an artificial intelligence platform for shipbuilding. The next-generation marine platform is intended to create an autonomous manufacturing system that links ship design, production, supply chain management and maintenance, while allowing shipyards to push design changes and real-time production updates to the shop floor and across suppliers. HD KSOE President and CEO Kim Hyung-kwan said the company aims to build a future-ready shipyard by combining AI, digital twin and automation technologies, while Siemens President and CEO Tony Hemmelgarn said the system could help improve throughput, quality and workforce productivity. The signing took place in Washington on Thursday on the sidelines of the opening ceremony of the Korea-U.S. Shipbuilding Partnership Center, which was established to support the MASGA initiative. Under that program, Seoul has agreed to invest US$150 billion to help revive the U.S. shipbuilding industry as part of a broader $350 billion investment commitment to the United States.

data.title: South Korea Vows Measures To Ease Inflation, Unveil Anti-Price Gouging Steps Next Month
data.description: According to Yonhap, South Korea will intensify efforts to curb upward pressure on consumer prices amid renewed geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, and Finance Minister Koo Yun-cheol said detailed steps to tackle unfair market practices will be announced next month. Koo made the remarks at a meeting on consumer prices with relevant ministries, saying the government will stay alert as escalating volatility in global oil prices could strain prices and supply chains. He said South Korea aims to bring consumer inflation, which has remained above 3% recently, down to the 2% range in July. Consumer prices rose 3.2% in June from a year earlier, the sharpest increase since December 2023, when the rate was also 3.2%. Koo also said the government will seek to improve the effectiveness of inflation-control measures, propose revisions to the Price Stabilization Act in August to strengthen penalties for hoarding and allow earlier disposal of confiscated goods, and ensure expanded tariff-rate quota programs lead to an actual decline in consumer prices in the second half.

data.title: 1kx Says On-Chain Protocol Fees Fell 33% in the Second Quarter
data.description:
1kx said on-chain protocol fees decreased 33% year-over-year in the second quarter. According to NS3.AI, DEX fees dropped $625 million, or 57%, while perpetual contract and prediction market fees rose 22% year-over-year.

The firm also said Polymarket's single-quarter fees approached $100 million.

data.title: Anthropic Reportedly Leads OpenAI in ARR as Both Companies Post Strong Growth
data.description:
TickerTrends data shows Anthropic's annual recurring revenue reached about $7.41 billion in July, compared with OpenAI's roughly $4.13 billion, leaving a gap of about $3.28 billion. According to ChainCatcher, Anthropic's tracked ARR rose from $1.02 billion in January to $3.56 billion in April, $6.96 billion in June, and $7.41 billion in July.

OpenAI's tracked ARR increased from $2.14 billion in January to $3.3 billion in May and $4.13 billion in July. Anthropic's ARR grew 57.7% since the last reporting period, while OpenAI rose 65.2%, indicating that OpenAI's recent growth rate was slightly faster despite Anthropic's larger absolute revenue base.

data.title: STOCKS | Asian Stocks Fall as Brent Trades Above $100
data.description:
Asian stocks followed Wall Street lower as investors questioned whether the billions pouring into the artificial intelligence buildout will generate adequate returns, according to Bloomberg.

Brent traded above $100 a barrel.

data.title: Circle Mints 250 Million USDC on Solana for the Fourth Time in Two Days
data.description: Circle minted 250 million USDC on Solana, according to Onchain Lens monitoring. According to Odaily, this was the fourth 250 million USDC mint on Solana in the past two days, bringing total USDC issuance on the network to 72.01 billion.

data.title: Huatai Securities Sees AI Agent Commercialization Driving Inference Demand in 2026
data.description: Huatai Securities said 2026 AI development is shifting from large-model pre-training to the commercial rollout of AI agents, with inference computing demand entering a faster-growth phase. Jiemian News reported that the brokerage sees three investment themes: AI supply chain names as domestic computing capacity in China builds a closed loop more quickly, supernode interconnects and storage upgrades move in tandem, and the inflection point in inference demand becomes clearer; power and passive components as AI power consumption lifts prices and volumes for MLCCs, inductors, capacitors and power semiconductors; and self-reliance as localization of upstream manufacturing, equipment and parts accelerates, while advanced packaging gains more value.

data.title: Japan Seen Key to AI Energy Challenge by Top Environmental Fund
data.description: Asia's best-performing environmental fund has increased its exposure to Japan, according to Bloomberg, as it expects the country's technology sector to play a growing role in meeting the artificial intelligence industry's rising power demand.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Superstate Launches on CoinList's Passage for Tokenized Stock Integration
data.description: CoinList said fintech company Superstate has fully launched on its Passage platform. According to Foresight News, new banks, fintech companies, and wallet providers can use the Passage SDK to embed Superstate's tokenized stocks into their own applications. The company said the stocks offered by Superstate represent actual equity ownership rather than IOUs.

data.title: ARK Invest Buys Tesla, Circle Shares and Sells Figma, Robinhood
data.description: Cathie Wood's Ark Invest bought 160,151 Tesla shares on Thursday, along with 130,136 Circle shares and 48,377 Securitize shares. According to Odaily, Ark Invest also sold 976,368 Figma shares and 40,553 Robinhood shares.

data.title: CITIC Securities Says 2027 Pig Prices Could Improve as Industry Deleverages
data.description: CITIC Securities said pig prices stayed weak in the first half of 2026, leaving the sector deeply loss-making and showing wide cost differences among listed companies. According to 36Kr, the broker said industry losses are continuing, cash flow pressure remains high, policy constraints are tightening, and regulatory measures are being implemented. It expects capacity cuts to continue in the second half of 2026, with a better pricing cycle possible in 2027. The firm added that per-head market value for live hog companies is already at a relatively low level and reiterated its positive view on the sector.

data.title: Musk Wants Peer Review for Frontier AI Models Before Release
data.description: Elon Musk said he wants the most advanced AI models to undergo peer review before release, rather than government review. He acknowledged that cooperation among leading AI companies would require him and OpenAI CEO Sam Altman to set aside their differences, according to 36Kr, even though the two recently clashed sharply on social media.

data.title: Amazon requires sellers to label AI-generated people in product media
data.description: According to CNBC, Amazon is requiring third-party sellers to tag product images and videos that contain AI-generated people after New York passed a law mandating greater transparency around synthetic performers in ads. The company told sellers on Wednesday to add specific metadata keywords to images and A+ content before upload, and said it will add an indicator to listings where applicable. Amazon said the rule does not apply to TV, video game or movie characters, or to content featuring real people that has been altered using AI. The New York law took effect last month and requires companies to disclose when synthetic performers are used in place of human actors in advertising. Amazon also has been expanding its use of AI across its business, while third-party sellers increasingly use the technology to create text, images and other listing content.

data.title: Bitcoin Could Break Above $72,000 After Range Trading, Whale Says
data.description:
Whale trader “Set Ten Big Goals” said on X that he reopened long positions shortly after closing short positions because his medium- and long-term view had not changed. According to Odaily, he said Bitcoin’s key dividing zone in the last bull market was near $60,000, while mainstream mining costs are now concentrated in the $50,000 to $60,000 range.

He said Bitcoin briefly fell to $58,000 last month before quickly rebounding, which confirmed support in that area. He added that, absent systemic risk or major changes in fundamentals, the risk-reward profile for continuing to short at current levels is not attractive. After Bitcoin completes consolidation and turnover in the $58,000 to $63,000 range and reclaims the area near $66,000, he said the market will have conditions for further gains, and he did not rule out a strong-volume candle pushing Bitcoin above $72,000.

He also said U.S. stocks, especially AI-related sectors, are trading at relatively high valuations and may become more volatile. He added that Bitcoin’s correlation with U.S. stocks has dropped noticeably compared with previous cycles and said continued institutional inflows and the strengthening of Bitcoin’s asset attributes are helping it move in a more independent direction.

data.title: STOCKS | South Korea’s KOSPI Falls 1.55% as SK Hynix and Samsung Electronics Decline
data.description:
South Korea’s KOSPI index fell 1.55% intraday to 7000 points, with losses led by major chip stocks.

According to NS3.AI, SK Hynix fell 1.8%, while Samsung Electronics dropped 1.4%.
